hypothesis: Incorporating ensemble diversity regularization through architectural heterogeneity—by combining structurally distinct GNN architectures (e.g., message-passing networks with different aggregation schemes) within the coupled ensemble rather than using identically-initialized homogeneous models—will amplify the knowledge distillation effect observed in this paper, such that individual members of a heterogeneous coupled ensemble will achieve lower prediction error on molecular property tasks than individual members of a homogeneous coupled ensemble of equivalent total parameter count, because increased inter-member functional diversity will produce higher-quality consensus targets that encode complementary inductive biases about molecular structure.
